market_briefing.summary: Global markets are turbulent due to U.S.-Iran conflict and inflationary pressures. Oil prices remain volatile, while precious metals are rallying amid geopolitical uncertainty.
highlights: Escalating U.S.-Iran conflict and closure of the Strait of Hormuz have disrupted energy supplies, causing significant market volatility.
Brent crude oil prices eased to $106.90 per barrel after briefly surging to $119; WTI fell to $93.63.
Precious metals are rallying strongly, with Gold surging 1.83% to $4,690.10 per ounce and Silver up 3.98%.
Federal Reserve maintained interest rates at 3.5%-3.75% on March 18 and revised 2026 inflation forecasts higher to 2.7%.
Global stock indices are mixed; U.S. markets showing resilience while European and some Asian indices faced declines due to energy shocks.
